behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Real failure risk is at end-to-end pipeline/runtime boundaries: CSV line indexing, validator rejection, and durable DLQ write must stay consistent under actual pipeline execution. MOCK-MISS: this is not only local validation logic; it also requires handoff to DLQ with preserved row metadata. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: a mocked queue can miss serialization/metadata-loss issues that occur in real transport or real writer integration. Considered Integration, but rejected because the behavior text requires rows to "appear in the dead-letter queue" (durable sink behavior) and does not provide explicit in-process fake/stub constraints to disqualify System. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior is deterministic schema validation ("missing required fields"), with no AI/ML judgment or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline as a real process against real infra endpoints (e.g., test S3 + DLQ service). Feed a CSV containing known missing required fields at specific line numbers. Assert those rows are absent from normalized output and present in DLQ payloads with exact original row numbers.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is pure transformation logic for date parsing/normalization. MOCK-MISS: failure can be reproduced within one function/object boundary using input/output cases; no module-boundary interaction is required. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Considered Integration and rejected based on behavior text: "transformation correctly converts date strings... to a single canonical format" describes algorithmic conversion only, not cross-component orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit tests over representative and edge-case date strings (valid ISO/US/EU, ambiguous dates, invalid values) asserting exact canonical output or explicit validation error.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This is a performance/resource behavior tied to runtime characteristics. REAL-INFRA: timeout and memory-pressure failures are process/OS-level and often only emerge under real streaming, buffering, and DB/S3 I/O. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes typically underrepresent latency/backpressure and memory footprint, hiding real OOM/timeout failures. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML output dependency; this is deterministic pipeline throughput/resource handling.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline in a real process with production-like config and a 500k-row fixture, using real storage/database endpoints for I/O. Measure wall-clock completion versus configured timeout and monitor peak RSS/heap to assert no OOM and bounded memory growth.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real HTTP/service behavior and retry timing semantics. REAL-INFRA: transient 503 handling and backoff behavior are best validated against real request/response behavior (or fault-injected HTTP layer), not just mocked method calls. MOCK-HIDE: mocks can always return scripted sequences without surfacing SDK/network edge cases and timing behavior. Considered Integration and rejected because behavior explicitly states "S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error," which is a real service/HTTP boundary. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model judgment involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against an S3 endpoint/proxy that injects transient 503 responses before success. Assert retry count, increasing backoff intervals (within tolerance), and that the batch eventually succeeds without full-batch failure.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: This behavior crosses process lifecycle + persistent DB state boundaries. REAL-INFRA: restart semantics require real process stop/start and durable database effects. MOCK-HIDE: mocked repositories can miss transaction/isolation/idempotency bugs that only appear with real PostgreSQL constraints and commit timing. Considered Integration and rejected because behavior explicitly requires "pipeline is restarted mid-batch," which is subprocess/runtime behavior beyond in-process wiring. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Start a real pipeline run against test PostgreSQL, force termination mid-batch after a known subset commits, then restart. Assert final table has exactly one copy per source row (via primary key/unique key checks) and total count matches expected unique rows.
